results.detail: The system MUST accept text, priority, dur, and optional deadline on POST /api/tasks and persist the task.
The system MUST reject task creation with 400 when the text field is empty, null, or whitespace-only.
The system MUST reject task creation with 400 when priority is outside the valid range (P1–P4 or equivalent).
The system MUST reject task creation with 400 when dur is zero or negative.
The system MUST enforce entity limits on task creation, returning 403 when the user is at the active-task limit.
The system MUST enforce feature gates on task creation, returning 403 when the user's plan lacks the required feature.
The system MUST auto-assign a task to a project when a valid project name is provided in the request body.
The system MUST return the created task with all persisted fields in the response body.
The system MUST require valid JWT authentication on POST /api/tasks, returning 401 for missing/invalid tokens and 403 for tokens without the juggler app claim.
The system MUST accept optional scheduling fields on task creation and persist them.
The system MUST allow partial field update via PUT /api/tasks/:id — only supplied fields are changed, others remain unchanged.
The system MUST reject full update with 404 when the task ID does not exist.
The system MUST allow status-only update via PUT /api/tasks/:id/status with a valid status value.
The system MUST reject status update with 400 when the requested status transition is invalid per the state machine.
The system MUST allow re-enabling a disabled task via PUT /api/tasks/:id/re-enable, checking entity limits before re-activation.
The system MUST allow taking ownership of a calendar-sourced task via POST /api/tasks/:id/take-ownership.
The system MUST validate updated fields with the same schema rules as task creation.
The system MUST reject updates that would create an invalid state combination with 400 and a human-readable error.
The system MUST delete a task via DELETE /api/tasks/:id, removing it so subsequent GET returns 404.
The system MUST reject deletion with 404 when the task ID does not exist.
The system MUST cascade-delete recurring templates: pending instances hard-deleted, completed instances archived.
The system MUST allow creating a project with a unique name via POST /api/projects.
The system MUST reject project creation with a duplicate name.
The system MUST allow renaming a project via PUT /api/projects/:id.
The system MUST allow reordering projects via PUT /api/projects/reorder.
The system MUST auto-create a project during batch task creation when a referenced project name does not yet exist.
The system MUST allow assigning and updating a color on a project via PUT /api/projects/:id.
The system MUST allow transitioning a task from todo to wip with an optional time_remaining value.
The system MUST allow transitioning a task from wip to done, setting completed_at to the current timestamp.
The system MUST allow transitioning a task to paused from wip.
The system MUST allow transitioning a task to cancelled from todo, wip, or paused.
The system MUST allow transitioning a task to disabled (soft-delete) from any non-terminal status.
The system MUST cap the scheduler's effective duration at 720 minutes via effectiveDuration() in ConstraintSolver.js.
The system MUST support Google Calendar OAuth connection: GET /api/gcal/connect redirects to Google OAuth.
The system MUST support Google Calendar disconnection, removing stored tokens and unlinking synced events.
The system MUST support Microsoft Calendar OAuth connection (plan-gated: calendar.microsoft).
The system MUST support Microsoft Calendar disconnection, removing stored tokens and unlinking synced events.
The system MUST support Apple Calendar CalDAV connection: POST /api/apple-cal/connect accepts CalDAV credentials.
The system MUST support Apple Calendar disconnection, removing stored credentials.
The system MUST push local tasks to the external calendar and pull external events to the local task store.
The system MUST fail loud on sync errors: no local task is silently overwritten.
The system MUST render a daily calendar view as a time-proportional grid.
The system MUST render a weekly calendar view showing all seven days.
The system MUST render a three-day calendar view showing the current day plus two following days.
The system MUST render a horizontal timeline view showing tasks chronologically.
The system MUST render a list view showing tasks in a simple chronological list format.
The system MUST render an S-curve view with dual 24h clocks.
The system MUST render a priority (kanban) view organizing tasks into P1–P4 columns.
The system MUST render a dependency graph view showing tasks as nodes with directional arrows.
The system MUST support drag-and-drop rescheduling on the calendar grid.
The system MUST support drag-and-drop priority change in the priority kanban view.
The system MUST support drag-and-drop dependency creation in the dependency graph view.
The system MUST allow declaring predecessor tasks via dependsOn on a non-recurring task.
The system MUST place a dependent task only after all its predecessors are placed.
The system MUST detect circular dependencies and flag affected tasks on the unscheduled/issues list.
The system MUST reject adding dependsOn to a recurring task with 400.
The system MUST reject converting a non-recurring task with existing dependsOn to recurring.
The system MUST place fixed tasks at their exact time in Phase 0 before any other task.
The system MUST sort remaining tasks by (slack ascending, priority ascending, duration descending, id).
The system MUST support 6 mutually exclusive placement modes.
The system MUST treat recurring as orthogonal to placement mode.
The system MUST execute placement through 7 logical phases.
The system MUST apply a 4-level fallback ladder for congested slots.
The system MUST respect time blocks.
The system MUST respect location constraints.
The system MUST respect tool constraints.
The system MUST respect weather constraints. Currently fail-open (weatherOk returns true when data missing).
The system MUST insert travel buffers between consecutive tasks at different locations.
The system MUST handle task splitting.
The system MUST handle dependency chains.
The system MUST expand recurring tasks for a 14-day horizon.
The system MUST apply delta writes.
The system MUST flag tasks that cannot be placed with _unplacedReason.
The system MUST enforce schedule floor and ceiling.
The system MUST return the complete schedule with both placed and unplaced items.
The system MUST handle time_window mode.
The system MUST handle all_day mode.
The system MUST handle reminder mode.
The system MUST handle anytime mode.
The system SHOULD generate time reports comparing estimated versus actual time.
The system SHOULD generate project burn-down reports.
The system SHOULD generate capacity planning reports.
The system MUST accept natural-language task commands via POST /api/ai/command.
The system MUST enforce a daily quota of 50 AI commands per user.
The system MUST enforce a rate limit of 2 AI requests per minute per user.
The system MUST provide emoji/icon suggestions via GET /api/tasks/suggest-icon.
The system MUST feature-gate AI natural-language commands behind the ai.natural_language_commands flag.
The system MUST authenticate all user-facing API requests using RS256 JWT tokens.
The system MUST reject requests without a valid JWT with 401.
The system MUST reject requests with a valid JWT that lacks the juggler app claim with 403.
The system SHOULD expose 20 MCP tools covering task CRUD, status updates, scheduling, etc.
The system MUST scope all MCP operations to the authenticated user.
The system MUST support daily recurrence.
The system MUST support weekly recurrence.
The system MUST support biweekly recurrence.
The system MUST support monthly recurrence.
The system MUST support interval recurrence.
The system MUST support rolling recurrence.
The system MUST expand recurring instances for a 14-day horizon.
The system MUST enforce the limits.recurring_templates entity limit.
The system MUST support a split toggle on any task, enabling chunked placement.
The system MUST enforce a minimum chunk size of splitMinDefault (default 15 minutes).
The system MUST merge runt chunks (below splitMin) into the previous chunk.
The system MUST day-lock recurring rigid splits.
The system MUST allow non-recurring splits to cross day boundaries.
The system MUST apply travel buffers only to the first and last ordinal of a split task.
The system MUST flag partial splits with partial_split in the unplaced list.
The system MUST allow users to define named locations with coordinates.
The system MUST allow assigning a location to a task via the location_id field.
The system MUST insert travel time buffers between consecutive tasks at different locations.
The system MUST enforce the limits.locations entity limit.
The system MUST allow users to define time block configurations by day type.
The system MUST support location schedule mapping.
The system MUST enforce the limits.schedule_templates entity limit.
The system MUST export all user data as a JSON payload via GET /api/data/export.
The system MUST support import with merge mode.
The system MUST support import with replace mode.
The system MUST validate import payloads against a schema.
The system MUST feature-gate both export and import operations.
The system MUST support batch task creation of up to 500 tasks.
The system MUST support batch task update of up to 2000 tasks.
The system MUST retry batch operations on deadlock.
The system MUST auto-create projects referenced in batch task creation.
The system MUST enforce feature gates at the route level.
The system MUST enforce entity limits.
The system MUST enforce array-membership features via requireFeatureIncludes middleware.
The system MUST expose the user's current plan, features, and limits.
The system MUST enforce downgrade.
The system MUST process billing webhooks with HMAC-SHA256 signature verification.
The system MUST provide geocoding via GET /api/weather/geocode.
The system MUST provide reverse geocoding via GET /api/weather/reverse-geocode.
The system MUST provide current weather conditions via GET /api/weather.
The system MUST provide daily weather forecasts via GET /api/weather.
The system MUST display weather badges on calendar views.
The system MUST support fixed placement mode for non-recurring tasks.
The system MUST allow unfixing a task.
The system MUST block recurring+fixed combination.
The system MUST lock calendar-synced tasks to fixed mode.
The system MUST allow users to define named tools via PUT /api/config.
The system MUST support a tool matrix mapping via PUT /api/config.
The system MUST respect tool constraints during scheduling.
The system MUST allow admin users to start impersonating another user.
The system MUST allow stopping impersonation via POST /api/impersonation/stop.
The system MUST audit all impersonation events and expose a log.
The system MUST allow disabling a task (soft-delete) by setting its status to disabled.
The system MUST allow re-enabling a disabled task via PUT /api/tasks/:id/re-enable.
The system MUST cascade-disable recurring template instances when the template is disabled.
The system MUST detach a calendar-sourced task from its provider link.
The system MUST set placement_mode to anytime after take-ownership.
The system MUST expose all user configuration keys via GET /api/config.
The system MUST allow updating a specific configuration key via PUT /api/config/:key.
The system MUST support user timezone preference.
The system MUST support calendar grid zoom level, split defaults, schedule floor/ceiling, and font size.
The system MUST trigger a scheduler re-run when time-related configuration changes.
The system MUST handle done status on a recurring instance.
The system MUST handle skip status on a recurring instance.
The system MUST handle cancel status on a recurring instance.
The system MUST auto-apply missed status to past recurring instances.
The system MUST soft-skip a recurring instance on delete.
The system MUST cascade-delete a recurring template.
The system MUST advance the rollingAnchor when a rolling instance is marked done.
The system MUST advance the rollingAnchor when a rolling instance is marked skip.
The system MUST nudge the rollingAnchor forward when a rolling instance is marked missed.
The system MUST enforce a stale guard: rolling anchor MUST NOT regress.
The system MUST backfill a null rollingAnchor from spacing history at scheduler start.
The system MUST support TimesPerCycle as an overlay on recurrence types.
The system MUST treat TPC instances as flexible when timesPerCycle < selectedDayCount.
The system MUST implement the keep fill policy.
The system MUST implement the backfill fill policy.
The system MUST enforce a spacing guard preventing TPC instances from clustering.
The system MUST expose the split toggle for ALL tasks in the UI.
The system MUST time-box recurring splits.
The system MUST allow non-recurring splits to cross day boundaries.
The system MUST pre-materialize recurring split chunks as DB rows in Phase 1.
The system MUST expand non-recurring splits inline in Phase 2.
The system MUST flag recurring splits that overflow their time-box.
The system MUST propagate deadlines backward through dependency chains. Implemented but imprecise.
The system SHOULD apply a capacity-aware offset to propagated deadlines. Not implemented.
The system MUST remove or replace the deadlineMisses array. Currently dead code.
The system MUST enforce earliest_start_at as a hard lower bound. No dedicated test.
The system MUST validate earliestStart <= deadline. No dedicated test.
The system SHOULD rename the field from start_after_at to earliest_start_at.
The system MUST treat weather as a hard constraint. Currently fail-open.
The system MUST flag weather-constrained tasks with _unplacedReason: weather_unavailable.
The system MUST display a weather data unavailable indicator in the frontend.
The system MUST extract hasWeatherConstraint into a shared module.
The system MUST resolve eligible windows from date to time blocks to windows by tag.
The system MUST filter eligible windows by task.when tags.
The system MUST resolve location using a 6-level priority chain.
The system MUST check tool availability via location to tool matrix.
The system MUST check weather conditions via the constraint chain.
The system MUST implement flexWhen as a boolean flag. No dedicated test.
The system MUST retry placement as anytime when flexWhen is true. No dedicated test.
The system MUST flag entries placed via FlexWhen relaxation with _flexWhenRelaxed. No dedicated test.
The system MUST route all scheduler triggers through enqueueScheduleRun.
The system MUST debounce scheduler triggers with a 2-second window.
The system MUST enforce a rate limit of 10 scheduler runs per minute per user.
The system MUST NOT trigger the scheduler recursively.
The system MUST skip scheduler triggers for non-scheduling field updates.
The system MUST provide an immediate health check at GET /api/health/immediate. No dedicated test.
The system MUST provide a full health check at GET /api/health/. No dedicated test.
The system MUST provide a detailed per-service health check. No dedicated test.
The system MUST expose feature event analytics. No dedicated test.
The system MUST expose Google Calendar connection status via GET /api/gcal/status.
The system MUST allow toggling Google Calendar auto-sync.
The system MUST expose Microsoft Calendar connection status.
The system MUST allow toggling Microsoft Calendar auto-sync.
The system MUST expose Apple Calendar connection status.
The system MUST allow selecting a single Apple Calendar for sync.
The system MUST allow selecting multiple Apple Calendars for sync.
The system MUST list available Apple Calendars.
The system MUST refresh the Apple Calendar list.
The system MUST allow updating an Apple Calendar's metadata.
The system MUST allow toggling Apple Calendar auto-sync.
The system MUST provide read-only schedule placements via GET /api/schedule/placements.
The system MUST allow enqueuing a scheduler nudge via POST /api/schedule/nudge.
The system MUST provide an admin-only debug scheduler endpoint. No dedicated test.
The system MUST provide an admin-only stepper session start. No dedicated test.
The system MUST provide a stepper session summary. No dedicated test.
The system MUST serve individual stepper steps. No dedicated test.
The system MUST allow stopping a stepper session. No dedicated test.
The system MUST expose impersonation targets via GET /api/impersonation/targets.
The system MUST allow ingesting weather data via POST /api/weather/ingest.
The system MUST expose a task data version via GET /api/tasks/version. No dedicated test.
The system MUST list disabled tasks via GET /api/tasks/disabled. No dedicated test.
The system MUST allow deleting a project via DELETE /api/projects/:id.
The system MUST list all user-defined tools via GET /api/tools/.
The system MUST allow replacing the entire tool inventory via PUT /api/tools/.
